name: evo-featureflags-client
documentation:
FeatureFlags client library for Python

See ``examples`` directory for complete examples for some major Web frameworks.

Overview
--------

Client supports Python >=3.9.


Installation
------------

To install package:

- ``pdm add evo-featureflags-client``

To release package:

- ``lets release 0.4.0 --message="Added feature"``

Development
-----------

Install dependencies:

- ``pdm install -d``

Pre-commit

``./scripts/enable-hooks.sh``

``./scripts/disable-hooks.sh``

TODO:

documentation: Values object to access current feature values state.

classes.name: featureflags_client.http.client.FeatureFlagsClient

methods.name: featureflags_client.http.client.FeatureFlagsClient.flags
documentation:
Context manager to wrap your request handling code and get actual
flags values.

methods.name: featureflags_client.http.client.FeatureFlagsClient.values
documentation:
Context manager to wrap your request handling code and get actual
feature values.

methods.name: featureflags_client.http.client.FeatureFlagsClient.preload
documentation:
Preload flags and values from featureflags server.
This method syncs all flags and values with server

documentation: Async version of `preload` method
Feature flags and values http based client.

classes.name: featureflags_client.http.flags.Flags
documentation: Flags object to access current flags state.

classes.name: featureflags_client.http.managers.dummy.DummyManager

methods.name: featureflags_client.http.managers.dummy.DummyManager.get
documentation: So that `featureflags.http.flags.Flags` will use default values.

Dummy feature flags manager.

It can be helpful when you want to use flags with their default values.

Dummy feature flags manager for asyncio apps.

It can be helpful when you want to use flags with their default values.
